dimension_explanations.alignment: Darul Qasim shows a medium donor fit by serving a specific academic niche within the Muslim community and addressing global education needs [6]. Its Zakat-eligible status and clear policy on asnaf categories provide a structured pathway for religious giving [3][7].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through advanced religious training and has demonstrated strong financial health with 29.3 months of working capital [1][2]. While it maintains a program expense ratio of 51.9%, its low fundraising cost of $0.02 per $1 raised ensures that donor contributions are processed with minimal overhead [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Darul Qasim is an Islamic institution of higher learning dedicated to preserving and imparting traditional knowledge derived from the Prophethood of Muhammad.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.02 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity position with 29.3 months of working capital
Established specialized curriculum in Islamic epistemology and jurisprudence
baseline_narrative.summary: Based in Illinois, Darul Qasim facilitates religious education and Arabic language studies through advanced programs such as the Iftā Program and Takhaṣṣuṣ fī al-Tafsīr [1][4]. The organization reported a total revenue of $2,696,418 and maintains a Zakat-eligible status to support its educational mission [1][3].

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="2">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, far outperforming industry benchmarks for cost-effective development.
Darul Qasim maintains <cite id="2">29.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive buffer against economic volatility and ensuring academic continuity.
The institution offers rare, advanced programs such as <cite id="4">Takhaṣṣuṣ fī al-Tafsīr</cite> and <cite id="5">Advanced Islamic Law</cite> that are not commonly available in Western settings.

strengths_deep_dive: Darul Qasim's financial stability is a cornerstone of its institutional model. With <cite id="1">total assets exceeding $10.2 million</cite> and a <cite id="1">net asset growth from $4.9M to $6.1M</cite> over three years, the organization has built a solid foundation for its 'Shaykh al-Hind' immersion programs. This growth is supported by a <cite id="1">diversified revenue stream</cite> that includes <cite id="1">$835,237 in program service revenue</cite>, indicating a healthy mix of tuition and philanthropic support. Furthermore, the <cite id="2">100% independent board</cite> ensures that governance remains objective and focused on the long-term mission of preserving Islamic epistemology.
rich_narrative.summary: Darul Qasim is an Illinois-based institution of higher learning that serves as a vital hub for traditional Islamic knowledge, offering a <cite id="4">specialized curriculum in Islamic epistemology</cite> and jurisprudence. The organization operates with a mission to <cite id="6">facilitate the learning of knowledge derived from Prophethood</cite>, bridging the gap between classical Islamic sciences and contemporary academic discourse. Financially, the institution reported <cite id="1">total revenue of $3,290,091</cite> in the 2023 tax year, demonstrating a significant <cite id="1">3-year revenue CAGR of 23.4%</cite>. While the organization maintains a <cite id="2">program expense ratio of 51.9%</cite>, it exhibits exceptional fiscal discipline in its development efforts, maintaining a <cite id="2">fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised</cite>. This lean fundraising model allows the institution to preserve its <cite id="2">29.3 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring long-term stability for its advanced academic tracks, including the <cite id="4">Iftā Program and Takhaṣṣuṣ fī al-Tafsīr</cite>. As a <cite id="3">zakat-eligible institution</cite>, Darul Qasim provides a structured pathway for donors to support students of knowledge through categories such as <cite id="3">fuqara, masakin, and gharimin</cite>.
